user.title: Set up Bermuda Tuner
user.description: This helper reads Bermuda scanner history, explains what looks wrong, and can calculate safer tuning values. Calculations run locally. Optional AI only receives a redacted report through your chosen Home Assistant conversation agent.

data.ai_enabled: Let the selected conversation agent explain redacted reports
data.conversation_agent: Conversation agent to use for optional explanations

abort.bermuda_required: Install and configure Bermuda before adding Bermuda Tuner.

init.title: What do you want to fix?
init.description: Start with coverage. Then calibrate one scanner at one metre, test the same scanner farther away, and only then balance multiple scanners.

menu_options.distance: Tune distance using a measured test

audit.title: Check scanner coverage first
audit.description: This reads Bermuda history and looks for missing scanner overlap, stale scanner relationships, and unstable signal history. No settings are changed.

one_meter.title: Find the one-metre signal value
one_meter.description: Put one beacon exactly one metre from one scanner. Keep both still for at least 30 seconds, then submit. The result is the value to consider for Bermuda ref_power.

data.device_address: Beacon address or name filter, optional

distance.title: Tune distance using a measured test
distance.description: Use the same beacon and scanner from the one-metre test. Move the beacon to a measured distance, usually 4 to 6 metres, keep it still, then submit. This estimates Bermuda attenuation.

data.device_address: Beacon address or name filter, optional
data.measured_distance: Actual distance from scanner in metres
data.reference_power: One-metre signal value from the previous step

balance.title: Balance scanners against each other
balance.description: Use this after basic distance tuning. Put one beacon where several scanners can hear it and keep it still. The result suggests per-scanner rssi_offsets so loud and quiet scanners compare more fairly.

data.device_address: Beacon address or name filter, optional

walk_test.title: Check which scanner wins here
walk_test.description: Stand at a real-world test point, wait for readings to settle, then submit. The result shows the strongest scanner, runner-up, and whether the margin is too close to trust.

data.device_address: Beacon address or name filter, optional
data.ambiguity_db: How close is too close, in dB

explain.title: Explain the current Bermuda settings
explain.description: Shows what the current smoothing, velocity, radius, update interval, and away timeout mean in normal language.

apply.title: Advanced: paste exact Bermuda settings
apply.description: This is for manual changes only. Paste an object with documented Bermuda option names, such as ref_power, attenuation, rssi_offsets, smoothing_samples, max_velocity, max_area_radius, update_interval, or devtracker_nothome_timeout. Nothing is applied until the confirmation screen.

confirm_apply.title: Confirm advanced settings change
confirm_apply.description:
Review these changes. Bermuda Tuner saves a rollback snapshot before applying them.

{result}

rollback.title: Undo the last settings change
rollback.description: Restore the latest Bermuda settings snapshot saved by Bermuda Tuner. This only affects changes applied through this tuner.

ai.title: Optional AI explanation settings
ai.description: Calculations always remain deterministic. When enabled, Ask AI sends only a redacted audit to the selected Home Assistant conversation agent. The tuner stores no AI transcript, but the provider privacy policy still applies.
